_source: Home systems & major appliances: the National Association of Home Builders / Bank of America Home Equity 'Study of Life Expectancy of Home Components' (2007) is the standard, widely-cited baseline for U.S. residential component lifespans (HVAC ~15-20yr, water heater ~10-15yr tank, asphalt-shingle roof ~20-25yr, aluminum gutters ~20yr, major kitchen/laundry appliances ~9-15yr); figures are cross-checked against InterNACHI's 'Standard Estimated Life Expectancy Chart' for home inspectors and Consumer Reports appliance-reliability lifespan data. Vehicles: average U.S. vehicle age at scrappage / typical useful life is commonly cited around 12 years by S&P Global Mobility (formerly IHS Markit) and the U.S. Bureau of Transportation Statistics; a 10-15 year range is used here to cover body styles and usage patterns. Replacement costs are ballpark 2024-2025 U.S. national averages: systems/appliances from Angi and HomeAdvisor cost guides; vehicles from Kelley Blue Book / Cox Automotive average new-transaction-price bands by segment. All figures are rough planning ranges, not quotes — local market, brand, and size vary widely.
